---
title: "Track: Getting Started with R"
description: "A beginner learning path for students with no prior programming experience who want to use R for data analysis."
listing:
  - id: related-r-resources
    contents: "../resources/*/*.qmd"
    include:
      categories: "R"
    type: table
    sort: "date asc"
    fields: [title, description, date]
---

## Who this track is for

This track is for students who have never programmed before and want to learn R for data analysis. By the end of the track, you will be able to load, clean, and visualize data in R using the tidyverse.

No prior experience with R or any other programming language is assumed. A working computer and a willingness to make mistakes are all you need.

---

## Core sequence

Work through these resources in order. Each page builds on the previous one.

1. **[Getting Started with R](../resources/r-programming/getting-started-with-r.qmd)**
   Install R and RStudio, understand the interface, and run your first commands. Start here if you have never opened R before.

*Additional pages will be added to this track as the hub grows.*

---

## Related resources

All hub resources tagged with `R`:

::: {#related-r-resources}
:::
